content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine, code-verified bugs. Amendment fr-6-2b17af9b: the stage event is durably committed (engine.rs:2656-2695) before record_planned_elsewhere_amendments() runs (2697-2712), and that helper silently swallows all failures (7555-7658), creating a window where mappings are permanently lost. Amendment fr-6-f0e1a47f: mapped_to_bead_id undergoes only JSON type deserialization (final_review.rs:474-513), and the restart decision at lines 1503-1556 uses .is_none() which returns false for Some(""), allowing empty/garbage model output to suppress the reopen/fix loop. Amendment fr-6-bf0488dc: comments are posted and flushed (success_reconciliation.rs:733-748) before verified mapping rows are written (755-779), and comment_bead() (br_process.rs:601-605) has no deduplication, so a crash in that window causes duplicate comments on replay.

## Votes

- `fr-6-2b17af9b`: Accept
  Verified against source. engine.rs commits the stage_completed journal event at lines 2656-2695 and only then calls record_planned_elsewhere_amendments() at lines 2697-2712 (and similarly at 3081-3090 for round-advance). The helper function at lines 7550-7660 returns void and logs-and-continues on every failure path: project record read failure (7555-7571), missing task_source (7574-7587), invalid milestone ID (7589-7606), and individual mapping write failure (7643-7658). Success reconciliation at lines 686-704 only loads whatever mappings currently exist and has no reconstruction logic. A crash or transient storage error between the stage commit and the mapping writes permanently drops the planned-elsewhere mapping with no recovery path, even though the run proceeds to completion. This is a genuine durability defect.
- `fr-6-f0e1a47f`: Accept
  Verified against source. mapped_to_bead_id is deserialized as Option<String> via serde_json::from_value at final_review.rs:474 with no semantic validation beyond JSON type checking. The restart decision at lines 1503-1505 and 1554-1556 uses .is_none(), which returns false for Some(""), Some(" "), or any garbage string from model output. This means an empty or whitespace-only mapped_to_bead_id incorrectly suppresses restart_required, skipping the reopen/fix loop. The engine at lines 7610-7618 records the value verbatim via as_deref(), passing through the invalid ID. Since this is a trust boundary with LLM output (model-generated reviewer payloads), empty/garbage strings are a realistic input. The fix — trimming and treating empty/whitespace-only values as None — is straightforward and necessary.
- `fr-6-bf0488dc`: Accept
  Verified against source. In success_reconciliation.rs, verify_and_comment_mappings() is called at line 714, which posts comments via br_mutation.comment_bead() (planned_elsewhere.rs:305-333). Then sync_flush() runs at lines 736-748. Only afterward, at lines 755-779, are verified mapping rows written via record_planned_elsewhere_mapping(). comment_bead() at br_process.rs:601-605 is a direct pass-through with no deduplication. If the process crashes after flush succeeds but before the verified mapping is persisted, replay sees the mapping as unverified (filtered at planned_elsewhere.rs:278 by mapped_bead_verified == false), re-runs verify_and_comment_mappings(), and posts the same comment again. The fix should either write the verified mapping before flushing comments or add idempotency to the comment posting path.
